prompt:
Write the DF-7 requirement PRD for the SWITCHBOARD STATE PLANE. Invoke the `appydave:spec-writer` skill. This is a SPEC-WRITING job only — produce a document, build nothing. Do NOT interview (run autonomously); where a decision is needed, draft a recommendation AND flag it inline as **OPEN DECISION (David)** so Marshall can surface it. Report ONE line and STOP.

=== THE REQUIREMENT (brainstorm to structure) ===
Dark Factory's job state currently lives in FLAT FILES on the factory floor: experiments/watchtower-engine/{queue,running,done,reports}/ with an atomic-rename claim (bin/claim-next.sh — rename(2) mutex, anonymous claim, ownership NOT recorded). This is a single-Marshall, single-folder scaffold. It breaks for TWO goals David wants: (1) PARALLEL ORCHESTRATION — multiple Marshall sessions need to run without merging work-state; cloning the repo to race shared running/ files is fork/merge-hell ('a disaster in normal programming' — David, do NOT design that). (2) FACTORY PORTABILITY — the factory must operate on apps in OTHER folders, not only by cd-ing out of dark-factory. The fix for BOTH is ONE capability: a SERVICE-BACKED SHARED-STATE PLANE so Marshall instances become THIN STATELESS CLIENTS talking to ONE stateful bus. State home = Switchboard (job pool/claims/ownership), AngelEye (liveness/who's-alive), AppyCtrl (dead-process reaping — unbuilt), Watchtower (view). See dark-factory memories constellation-first-placement, dark-factory-is-a-constellation-of-apps, appystack-vs-sentinel.

=== GROUNDED FACTS (verified 2026-06-09 — build ON these, do NOT propose rebuilding Switchboard) ===
Switchboard = an AppySentinel RECIPE-APP at ~/dev/ad/apps/switchboard (launchd com.appydave.switchboard, ports :5099/:5100; built on @appydave/appysentinel-core). Capabilities are added as RECIPES in named planes — see appysentinel.json: input[poll-command], storage[snapshot-store], interface[api-binding(HTTP)+mcp-binding(MCP)], transport[sse-deliver(SSE)], coordination[staleness-detector]. It ALREADY has: a /jobs POST endpoint (src/access/bindings/api-binding.ts:170), a /health GET, a queue/ dir, and a working coordination recipe (src/coordination/staleness-detector.ts) which is THE MODEL to copy. Storage today is only snapshot-store (ephemeral process snapshots). READ these files before writing: ~/dev/ad/apps/switchboard/appysentinel.json, src/access/bindings/api-binding.ts, src/coordination/staleness-detector.ts, src/coordination/index.ts, src/storage/snapshot-store.ts, README.md, CLAUDE.md. Also read the CLIENT side that must become a Switchboard client: ~/dev/ad/apps/dark-factory/experiments/watchtower-engine/bin/{claim-next.sh,dispatch-swagger.sh,reaper.sh}. And the related already-written spec DF-3 (backlog/specs/stability-1-instrument-loop-spec.md — 'observe the loop externally via Switchboard') which this MUST cohere with, not duplicate.

=== SHAPE OF THE BUILD (the PRD must specify, modeled on staleness-detector) ===
Approx TWO new recipes: (a) a durable STORAGE recipe holding job-state — pool/claimed/running/done + ownership (claimed_by) + timestamps, surviving restart; (b) a COORDINATION recipe arbitrating CLAIM-BY-ID (atomic, so two clients can't double-claim) + recording claimed_by, exposed through the EXISTING api-binding (HTTP) AND mcp-binding (MCP) so Marshall talks to it as an MCP tool. Liveness/orphan-reaping integrates AngelEye (GET :5051/.../liveness) — the reaper keys off liveness, not 'landed in done/'. Specify the MIGRATION/COEXISTENCE path from flat-files (don't big-bang) and how a thin client connects (the dark-factory engine's claim-next.sh becomes an API/MCP call).

=== PRD MUST COVER ===
Problem & goals (the two David goals, explicitly) · non-goals (NOT repo-cloning; NOT rebuilding Switchboard) · the recipe inventory (storage + coordination, named, modeled on staleness-detector) · job-state data model/schema · claim-by-id + ownership contract over HTTP+MCP · liveness/orphan-reaping via AngelEye · the thin-client contract (how dark-factory engine + future N Marshalls connect) · migration/coexistence from flat-files · open decisions (flagged for David) · acceptance criteria · build-via note (Ralphy in-app, switchboard repo). Stack-agnostic where possible; Switchboard specifics are fine since the target IS Switchboard.

=== DELIVER ===
Write the PRD to ~/dev/ad/apps/dark-factory/backlog/specs/df7-switchboard-state-plane-spec.md (Marshall authors requirements HERE; the in-app Ralphy build leg copies it into switchboard/docs/requirements/ later). Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping: write the run record, move this ticket to done/, and write the reports/ handback (DO NOT skip the reports/ handback). Report ONE line: the spec path + section count + how many OPEN DECISIONS you flagged for David.
